title: Quiz: Introduction to Quantum Machine Learning
description: Test your understanding of QML fundamentals and the quantum-classical interface

questions.question: Which of the following is the most NISQ-friendly approach to quantum machine learning?
questions.explanation: Variational quantum algorithms use shallow circuits and hybrid quantum-classical optimization, making them suitable for noisy intermediate-scale quantum (NISQ) devices.

questions.question: The 'barren plateau' problem in QML refers to:

options.text: Gradients vanishing exponentially with circuit depth

questions.explanation: Barren plateaus occur when the gradient of the cost function vanishes exponentially as circuits become deeper, making training impossible.

questions.question: For which type of data is quantum advantage most clearly established?
questions.explanation: Quantum computers have the clearest advantage when processing quantum data, as classical computers cannot efficiently represent quantum states.

questions.question: In a variational quantum algorithm, the quantum and classical components are used for:

options.text: Quantum: circuit execution, Classical: parameter optimization
Both components do the same task

questions.explanation: In the hybrid loop, the quantum computer executes parameterized circuits and measures outcomes, while the classical computer optimizes the parameters.

questions.question: Loading classical data into a quantum state is always efficient and doesn't affect potential quantum speedups.
questions.explanation: Data loading can be expensive and may eliminate quantum speedup. This 'data loading bottleneck' is a major challenge in QML.
